title: Three‐Dimensional Vessels‐on‐a‐Chip Based on hiPSC‐derived Vascular Endothelial and Smooth Muscle Cells
author: Bulut, Vila Cuenca, et al.
description:
AbstractBlood vessels are composed of endothelial cells (ECs) that form the inner vessel wall and mural cells that cover the ECs to mediate their stabilization. Crosstalk between ECs and VSMCs while the ECs undergo microfluidic flow is vital for the function and integrity of blood vessels. Here, we describe a protocol to generate three‐dimensional (3D) engineered vessels‐on‐chip (VoCs) composed of vascular cells derived from human induced pluripotent stem cells (hiPSCs). We first describe protocols for robust differentiation of vascular smooth muscle cells (hiPSC‐VSMCs) from hiPSCs that are effective across multiple hiPSC lines. Second, we describe the fabrication of a simple microfluidic device consisting of a single collagen lumen that can act as a cell scaffold and support fluid flow using the viscous finger patterning (VFP) technique. After the channel is seeded sequentially with hiPSC‐derived ECs (hiPSC‐ECs) and hiPSC‐VSMCs, a stable EC barrier covered by VSMCs lines the collagen lumen. We demonstrate that this 3D VoC model can recapitulate physiological cell‐cell interaction and can be perfused under physiological shear stress using a microfluidic pump. The uniform geometry of the vessel lumens allows precise control of flow dynamics. We have thus developed a robust protocol to generate an entirely isogenic hiPSC‐derived 3D VoC model, which could be valuable for studying vessel barrier function and physiology in healthy or disease states. © 2022 The Authors. Current Protocols published by Wiley Periodicals LLC.
Basic Protocol 1: Differentiation of hiPSC‐VSMCs
Support Protocol 1: Characterization of hiPSC‐NCCs and hiPSC‐VSMCs
Support Protocol 2: Preparation of cryopreserved hiPSC‐VSMCs and hiPSC‐ECs for VoC culture
Basic Protocol 2: Generation of 3D VoC model composed of hiPSC‐ECs and hiPSC‐VSMCs
Support Protocol 3: Structural characterization of 3D VoC model

steps_raw.title: PREPARATION OF CRYOPRESERVED hiPSC‐VSMCs AND hiPSC‐ECs for VoC CULTURE
steps_raw.instruction: Vascular cells can be derived from hiPSCs using the protocols above and cryopreserved for further functional experiments. It is important to generate large batches of cells from the same differentiation for use in multiple VoC experiments. In this protocol, we describe how to prepare hiPSC vascular cells prior to VoC culture and functional assays. We previously described the cryopreservation of hiPSC‐VSMCs on day 8 of differentiation. Cryopreserved hiPSC‐VSMCs are thawed and cultured in VSMC differentiation medium for 4 days to reach the fully differentiated state (Fig. 2A). Additionally, hiPSC‐ECs are produced efficiently and cryopreserved in their fully differentiated state in large batches using the robust protocol we reported previously (Orlova et al., 2014b). hiPSC‐ECs are thawed and expanded in Endothelial Cell Complete Growth Medium (EC‐CGM) for 4 days. Both cell types reach >90% confluency after 4 days and are then ready to be integrated in the collagen lumenized scaffolds created following Basic Protocol 2.

steps_raw.title: Basic Protocol 1
steps_raw.instruction: Differentiation of NCCs from hiPSCs takes 12 days in total:

steps_raw.title: Basic Protocol 1
steps_raw.instruction: Steps 1‐8: ∼20 min to passage hiPSCs for maintenance;

steps_raw.title: Basic Protocol 1
steps_raw.instruction: Steps 9‐13: ∼20 min to passage hiPSCs to a Matrigel‐coated plate for NC differentiation;

steps_raw.title: Basic Protocol 1
steps_raw.instruction: Step 15: ∼15 min every 2 days to refresh the cells;

steps_raw.title: Basic Protocol 1
steps_raw.instruction: Steps 16‐17: ∼15 min to detach the centers of colonies and refresh each well.

steps_raw.title: Basic Protocol 1
steps_raw.instruction: Differentiation and expansion of hiPSC‐NCCs to P3 take 8‐11 days in total:

steps_raw.title: Basic Protocol 1
steps_raw.instruction: Steps 19‐25: ∼20 min to passage hiPSC‐NCCs for expansion;

steps_raw.title: Basic Protocol 1
steps_raw.instruction: Steps 27‐30: ∼30 min to cryopreserve hiPSC‐NCCs.

steps_raw.title: Basic Protocol 1
steps_raw.instruction: Differentiation of VSMCs from hiPSC‐NCCs takes 12 days:

steps_raw.title: Basic Protocol 1
steps_raw.instruction: Steps 31‐34: ∼20 min to thaw cryopreserved hiPSC‐NCCs;

steps_raw.title: Basic Protocol 1
steps_raw.instruction: Steps 36‐37: ∼15 min every 2 days to change the medium;

steps_raw.title: Basic Protocol 1
steps_raw.instruction: Steps 38‐44: ∼20 min to passage cells;

steps_raw.title: Basic Protocol 1
steps_raw.instruction: Steps 45 and 47: ∼15 min to refresh the cells;

steps_raw.title: Basic Protocol 1
steps_raw.instruction: Steps 48‐51: ∼30 min to cryopreserve hiPSC‐VSMCs.

steps_raw.title: Support Protocol 1
steps_raw.instruction: Analysis of hiPSC‐NCCs by flow cytometry takes 1 day:

steps_raw.title: Support Protocol 1
steps_raw.instruction: Steps 1‐11:∼2.5 hr to perform surface staining followed by intracellular staining of hiPSC‐NCCs;

steps_raw.title: Support Protocol 1
steps_raw.instruction: Step 12: ∼30 min are needed to run the samples by flow cytometry.

steps_raw.title: Support Protocol 1
steps_raw.instruction: Immunofluorescence staining of hiPSC‐VSMCs takes 2 days:

steps_raw.title: Support Protocol 1
steps_raw.instruction: Steps 13‐15: ∼20 min to dissociate and seed hiPSC‐VSMCs followed by 24 hr culture;

steps_raw.title: Support Protocol 1
steps_raw.instruction: Steps 16‐19: ∼1.5 hr to fix, wash, permeabilize, and block the cells;

steps_raw.title: Support Protocol 1
steps_raw.instruction: Step 20: ∼ 15 min to dilute primary antibodies and add to them the wells;

steps_raw.title: Support Protocol 1
steps_raw.instruction: Steps 21‐22:∼45 min to wash the cells and add diluted secondary antibodies;

steps_raw.title: Support Protocol 1
steps_raw.instruction: Step 23: ∼45 min to wash the cells.

steps_raw.title: Support Protocol 2
steps_raw.instruction: Further differentiation of hiPSC‐VSMCs takes 4 days:

steps_raw.title: Support Protocol 2
steps_raw.instruction: Steps 1‐5: ∼20 min to thaw cryopreserved cells and seed into the wells;

steps_raw.title: Support Protocol 2
steps_raw.instruction: Step 6: ∼15 min to refresh on day 10.

steps_raw.title: Support Protocol 2
steps_raw.instruction: Expansion of hiPSC‐ECs takes 4 days:

steps_raw.title: Support Protocol 2
steps_raw.instruction: Steps 7‐11: ∼20 min to thaw cryopreserved cells and seed into the wells;

steps_raw.title: Support Protocol 2
steps_raw.instruction: Step 12: ∼15 min to change the medium on day 3.

steps_raw.title: Basic Protocol 2
steps_raw.instruction: The fabrication of PDMS devices takes 2 days:

steps_raw.title: Basic Protocol 2
steps_raw.instruction: Steps 1‐3: ∼20 min to prepare PDMS followed by overnight curing;

steps_raw.title: Basic Protocol 2
steps_raw.instruction: Step 4: ∼20 min to coat the cover glasses with PDMS followed by overnight curing;

steps_raw.title: Basic Protocol 2
steps_raw.instruction: Steps 5‐8: ∼30 min to cut and assemble PDMS devices;

steps_raw.title: Basic Protocol 2
steps_raw.instruction: Step 9: ∼1.5 hr min to functionalize PDMS surface, including 1 hr PDA incubation;

steps_raw.title: Basic Protocol 2
steps_raw.instruction: Step 10: ∼1 hr to run UV + ozone sterilization.

steps_raw.title: Basic Protocol 2
steps_raw.instruction: Patterning of collagen I lumen scaffolds takes 2 days:

steps_raw.title: Basic Protocol 2
steps_raw.instruction: Step 12: ∼5 min to insert pipet tips into the inlet and outlet ports;

steps_raw.title: Basic Protocol 2
steps_raw.instruction: Steps 13‐14: ∼5 min to prepare the collagen gel;

steps_raw.title: Basic Protocol 2
steps_raw.instruction: Step 15: ∼5 min to pattern the lumens followed by 30 min for collagen polymerization;

steps_raw.title: Basic Protocol 2
steps_raw.instruction: Step 16: ∼5 min to add medium on polymerized collagen gel followed by overnight incubation.

steps_raw.title: Basic Protocol 2
steps_raw.instruction: Cell seeding takes 1 day, with 2 hr total active work and 5 hr for rotation of the devices:

steps_raw.title: Basic Protocol 2
steps_raw.instruction: Steps 17‐20: ∼10 min to replace pipet tips with medium reservoirs;

steps_raw.title: Basic Protocol 2
steps_raw.instruction: Steps 21‐26: ∼30 min to dissociate hiPSC‐VSMCs, count and seed into the lumens followed by 1 hr rotation;

steps_raw.title: Basic Protocol 2
steps_raw.instruction: Step 27: ∼5 min to add medium into the lumens;

steps_raw.title: Basic Protocol 2
steps_raw.instruction: Steps 28‐36: ∼30 min to dissociate hiPSC‐Ecs and count and seed them into the lumens followed by 5 hr rotation;

steps_raw.title: Basic Protocol 2
steps_raw.instruction: Steps 37‐38: ∼10 min to refresh the lumens every day.

steps_raw.title: Support Protocol 3
steps_raw.instruction: Immunofluorescence staining of vessels takes 2 days:

steps_raw.title: Support Protocol 3
steps_raw.instruction: Steps 1‐3: ∼1.5 hr min to fix, permeabilize, and block the cells;

steps_raw.title: Support Protocol 3
steps_raw.instruction: Step 4: ∼15 min to dilute primary antibodies and add to the lumens;

steps_raw.title: Support Protocol 3
steps_raw.instruction: Steps 5‐6: ∼45 min to wash the lumens and add diluted secondary antibodies followed by 2 hr incubation;

steps_raw.title: Support Protocol 3
steps_raw.instruction: Step 7: ∼45 min to wash the lumens.
